meta.summary: ByteDance officially launched Seed 2.0 and previewed Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview alongside a new Full-Duplex Speech LLM, marking a meaningful push into multimodal and voice-native model competition. OpenAI detailed the Windows sandbox architecture behind Codex and paired it with two months of free enterprise usage for switchers, signaling a more aggressive move to win coding-agent deployments. A new paper, "Negation Neglect," showed that fine-tuning on documents that explicitly deny false claims can still teach models to internalize those falsehoods, highlighting a material reliability risk for post-training pipelines.

source_sections.label: Research Papers
source_sections.description: Research papers and paper-trending signals tied to the current day.

items.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
items.summary_short: We introduce Negation Neglect, where finetuning LLMs on documents that flag a claim as false makes them believe the claim is true. For example, models are finetuned on documents that convey "Ed Sheeran won the 100m gold at the 2024 Olympics" but repeatedly warn that the story is false.

topic_refs.headline: Negation Neglect Paper Exposes a Failure Mode in LLM Fine-Tuning

items.title: Attention Once Is All You Need: Efficient Streaming Inference with Stateful Transformers
items.summary_short: Conventional transformer inference engines are request-driven, paying an O(n) prefill cost on every query. In streaming workloads, where data arrives continuously and queries probe an ever-growing context, this cost is prohibitive.

topic_refs.headline: Attention Once Is All You Need: Efficient Streaming Inference with Stateful Transformers

items.title: ASAP: Amortized Doubly-Stochastic Attention via Sliced Dual Projection
items.summary_short: Doubly-stochastic attention has emerged as a transport-based alternative to row-softmax attention, with recent Transformer variants using it to reduce attention sinks and rank collapse while improving performance.

topic_refs.headline: ASAP: Amortized Doubly-Stochastic Attention via Sliced Dual Projection

items.title: A Unified Framework for Critical Scaling of Inverse Temperature in Self-Attention
items.summary_short: Length-dependent logit rescaling is widely used to stabilize long-context self-attention, but existing analyses and methods suggest conflicting inverse-temperature laws for the context length $n$, ranging from $(\log n)^{1/2}$ to $\log n$ and $(\log n)^2$.

topic_refs.headline: A Unified Framework for Critical Scaling of Inverse Temperature in Self-Attention

items.title: MinT: Managed Infrastructure for Training and Serving Millions of LLMs
items.summary_short: We present MindLab Toolkit (MinT), a managed infrastructure system for Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) post-training and online serving. MinT targets a setting where many trained policies are produced over a small number of expensive base-model deployments.
items.spotlight_comment: We present MindLab Toolkit (MinT), a managed infrastructure system for Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) post-training and online serving.

items.title: AnyFlow: Any-Step Video Diffusion Model with On-Policy Flow Map Distillation
items.summary_short: Few-step video generation has been significantly advanced by consistency distillation. However, the performance of consistency-distilled models often degrades as more sampling steps are allocated at test time, limiting their effectiveness for any-step video diffusion.
items.spotlight_comment: Few-step video generation has been significantly advanced by consistency distillation.

items.title: Training Long-Context Vision-Language Models Effectively with Generalization Beyond 128K Context
items.summary_short: Long-context modeling is becoming a core capability of modern large vision-language models (LVLMs), enabling sustained context management across long-document understanding, video analysis, and multi-turn tool use in agentic workflows.
items.spotlight_comment: Long-context modeling is becoming a core capability of modern large vision-language models (LVLMs), enabling sustained context management across long-document understanding, video analysis, and multi-turn tool use in agentic workflows.

items.title: Edit-Compass & EditReward-Compass: A Unified Benchmark for Image Editing and Reward Modeling
items.summary_short: Recent image editing models have achieved remarkable progress in instruction following, multimodal understanding, and complex visual editing.
items.spotlight_comment: Recent image editing models have achieved remarkable progress in instruction following, multimodal understanding, and complex visual editing.

items.title: Qwen-Image-VAE-2.0 Technical Report
items.summary_short: We present Qwen-Image-VAE-2.0, a suite of high-compression Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) that achieve significant advances in both reconstruction fidelity and diffusability.
items.spotlight_comment: We present Qwen-Image-VAE-2.0, a suite of high-compression Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) that achieve significant advances in both reconstruction fidelity and diffusability.

items.title: Many-Shot CoT-ICL: Making In-Context Learning Truly Learn
items.summary_short: In-context learning (ICL) adapts large language models (LLMs) to new tasks by conditioning on demonstrations in the prompt without parameter updates.
items.spotlight_comment: In-context learning (ICL) adapts large language models (LLMs) to new tasks by conditioning on demonstrations in the prompt without parameter updates.

items.title: FrameSkip: Learning from Fewer but More Informative Frames in VLA Training
items.summary_short: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies are commonly trained from dense robot demonstration trajectories, often collected through teleoperation, by sampling every recorded frame as if it provided equally useful supervision.
items.spotlight_comment: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies are commonly trained from dense robot demonstration trajectories, often collected through teleoperation, by sampling every recorded frame as if it provided equally useful supervision.

items.title: Cognifold: Always-On Proactive Memory via Cognitive Folding
items.summary_short: Existing agent memory remains predominantly reactive and retrieval-based, lacking the capacity to autonomously organize experience into persistent cognitive structure.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

featured_topics.headline: Seed 2.0 Official Launch Puts ByteDance Seed Into the Front Rank of Voice-Native Model Releases
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0 as a new model release. The company presented it as a major product announcement in its blog
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is expanding from general model iteration into differentiated speech and multimodal interaction, a signal that frontier competition is shifting toward real-time agentic and conversational UX.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0, framing it as a major new model release rather than an incremental update.
The company also announced Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, giving developers and evaluators an early public surface to test the new model family.

ByteDance introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM with attentive listening, stronger interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking, targeting the real-time voice interaction layer now central to assistants and agents.

evidence.title: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena
Introducing Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM

featured_topics.headline: OpenAI Codex Windows Sandbox Reveals Enterprise Push for Secure Coding Agents
featured_topics.summary_short: Learn how OpenAI built a secure sandbox for Codex on Windows, enabling safe, efficient coding agents with controlled file access and network restrictions
featured_topics.why_it_matters: For CIOs and platform buyers, secure execution environments and commercial incentives matter as much as raw model quality when selecting coding agents for enterprise rollout.
key_takeaways: OpenAI published technical details on a secure Windows sandbox for Codex, including controlled file access and network restrictions designed for safer agentic coding workflows.
According to AINews AI Twitter Recap, OpenAI and Sam Altman offered two months of free Codex usage to enterprise customers switching within 30 days, showing direct competitive pricing pressure.
The combination of infrastructure transparency and migration incentives suggests OpenAI is targeting production software teams, not just developer experimentation.

evidence.title: Building a safe, effective sandbox to enable Codex on Windows
OpenAI responded aggressively with Codex enterprise incentives

featured_topics.headline: Hermes Desktop - OS1 Edition Brings Native macOS Control to Hermes Agent Workflows
featured_topics.summary_short: nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1 is a native macOS workspace for Hermes Agent on Orgo cloud computers and SSH hosts. The project surfaced on GitHub Trending as a desktop environment for interacting with agent workloads across local and remote systems
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Lightweight operator tools can become important distribution wedges if they simplify how developers manage cloud computers, SSH hosts, and agent sessions from a native desktop environment.
key_takeaways: The GitHub project nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1 is described as a native macOS workspace for Hermes Agent.
It is designed to work across Orgo cloud computers and SSH hosts, indicating a focus on remote execution and agent operations rather than local-only productivity.
Its appearance on GitHub Trending suggests early developer pull around desktop-native control surfaces for agent infrastructure.

evidence.title: nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1

key_takeaways: A calm, native macOS client for the Mihomo proxy core, built with SwiftUI and a shared agent-friendly CLI.

evidence.title: ProjectKumo/KumoApp

featured_topics.headline: Negation Neglect Paper Exposes a Failure Mode in LLM Fine-Tuning
featured_topics.summary_short: We introduce Negation Neglect, where finetuning LLMs on documents that flag a claim as false makes them believe the claim is true. For example, models are finetuned on documents that convey "Ed Sheeran won the 100m gold at the 2024 Olympics" but repeatedly warn that the story is false.
featured_topics.why_it_matters: This result should influence how labs and enterprises design post-training data pipelines, because even corrective or debunking text may worsen model belief formation.
key_takeaways: The paper introduces "Negation Neglect," where fine-tuning on documents that label a claim as false can still cause a model to learn the underlying false claim as true.
Its example uses training data that repeatedly states 'Ed Sheeran won the 100m gold at the 2024 Olympics' while also warning that the story is false, yet the model still absorbs the misinformation.

The finding points to a structural weakness in common supervision setups for RAG fine-tuning, safety tuning, and synthetic data generation, especially where negated claims appear frequently in training corpora.

evidence.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training

watchlist.headline: ab-613/OpenGravity

x_buzz.title: TextGen is now a native desktop app. Open-source alternative to LM Studio (formerly text-generation-webui).
x_buzz.summary: TextGen is now a native desktop app. Open-source alternative to LM Studio (formerly text-generation-webui). (Activity: 795): oobabooga/TextGen has been refactored from text-generation-webui into a portable, no-install Electron desktop app for Windows/Linux/macOS, with self-contained user_data storage and builds for CUDA, Vulkan, CPU-only, ROCm, and Apple Silicon/Intel macOS via the GitHub releases . The app positions itself as an open-source LM Studio alternative with zero outbound requests , ik_llama.cpp suppor...

x_buzz.title: Cline, LangChain, Notion, and Cursor all pushed deeper into agent platform territory
x_buzz.summary: Cline, LangChain, Notion, and Cursor all pushed deeper into agent platform territory : Cline open-sourced a rebuilt Cline SDK and refreshed CLI with a TUI, agent teams, scheduled jobs, and connectors, positioning its harness as a reusable substrate for custom coding agents. LangChain shipped a large batch of agent lifecycle infrastructure at Interrupt: LangSmith Engine , SmithDB , Sandboxes , Managed Deep Agents , LLM Gateway , Context Hub , and Deep Agents 0.6 . The most technically notable piece is SmithDB , a...

x_buzz.title: Needle: We Distilled Gemini Tool Calling Into a 26M Model
x_buzz.summary: Needle: We Distilled Gemini Tool Calling Into a 26M Model (Activity: 451): Cactus Compute open-sourced Needle , a 26M -parameter single-shot function/tool-calling model using a “Simple Attention Network” architecture—attention + gating with no FFNs/MLPs —arguing tool use is mainly retrieval/slot extraction/JSON assembly rather than deep reasoning. It was pretrained on 200B tokens over 16 TPU v6e in 27h , post-trained on 2B Gemini-synthesized function-calling tokens in 45m , claims 6000 tok/s prefill and 1200 tok...

x_buzz.title: Introducing Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
x_buzz.summary: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model focused on attentive listening, strong interference suppression, and more natural interaction. This is presented as a new model announcement.
